result.stdout:
Description:
  Convert TRX files to Visual Studio Test Playlists

Usage:
  trx-to-vsplaylist [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  convert <trx-files>     Convert TRX file(s) to a Visual Studio Test Playlist
  merge <playlist-files>  Merge multiple playlist files into a single playlist with de-duplicated tests

commands.payload:
Description:
  Convert TRX files to Visual Studio Test Playlists

Usage:
  trx-to-vsplaylist [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  convert <trx-files>     Convert TRX file(s) to a Visual Studio Test Playlist
  merge <playlist-files>  Merge multiple playlist files into a single playlist with de-duplicated tests

result.stdout:
Description:
  Convert TRX file(s) to a Visual Studio Test Playlist

Usage:
  trx-to-vsplaylist convert <trx-files>... [options]

Arguments:
  <trx-files>  Path(s) to the TRX file(s) to convert. Multiple files can be specified to merge into a single playlist.

Options:
  -o, --output <output>    Path to the output playlist file or directory. If not specified, the playlist will be saved in the same directory as the first trx file with the same name but with .playlist extension.
  -f, --outcome <outcome>  Test outcomes to include (e.g. Passed Failed Skipped). Can be specified multiple times.
  --skip-empty             Do not write a playlist file if there are no tests in the playlist.
  --separate               When multiple TRX files are provided, create separate playlist files for each instead of merging into one. Output path must be a directory.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Convert TRX file(s) to a Visual Studio Test Playlist

Usage:
  trx-to-vsplaylist convert <trx-files>... [options]

Arguments:
  <trx-files>  Path(s) to the TRX file(s) to convert. Multiple files can be specified to merge into a single playlist.

Options:
  -o, --output <output>    Path to the output playlist file or directory. If not specified, the playlist will be saved in the same directory as the first trx file with the same name but with .playlist extension.
  -f, --outcome <outcome>  Test outcomes to include (e.g. Passed Failed Skipped). Can be specified multiple times.
  --skip-empty             Do not write a playlist file if there are no tests in the playlist.
  --separate               When multiple TRX files are provided, create separate playlist files for each instead of merging into one. Output path must be a directory.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Merge multiple playlist files into a single playlist with de-duplicated tests

Usage:
  trx-to-vsplaylist merge <playlist-files>... [options]

Arguments:
  <playlist-files>  Path(s) to the playlist file(s) to merge. Supports glob patterns (e.g., *.playlist, **/*.playlist). Tests will be automatically de-duplicated.

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  Path to the output merged playlist file. If not specified and all input files are in the same directory, the merged file will be created in that directory.
  --skip-empty           Do not write a playlist file if there are no tests in the merged playlist.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Merge multiple playlist files into a single playlist with de-duplicated tests

Usage:
  trx-to-vsplaylist merge <playlist-files>... [options]

Arguments:
  <playlist-files>  Path(s) to the playlist file(s) to merge. Supports glob patterns (e.g., *.playlist, **/*.playlist). Tests will be automatically de-duplicated.

Options:
  -o, --output <output>  Path to the output merged playlist file. If not specified and all input files are in the same directory, the merged file will be created in that directory.
  --skip-empty           Do not write a playlist file if there are no tests in the merged playlist.
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
